extracted.title: REMINDER: Mainline-200 Pigging (CorinSEG), March 5-14, 2026
extracted.description:
Columbia Gulf Transmission LLC (CGT) reminds customers of upcoming pigging on Maineline-200 from Corinth Compressor Station to Banner Compressor Station beginning on Thursday March 5, 2026, through Saturday, March 14, 2026. As a result, the backhaul capacity through CorinSEG will be reduced to a level between 2,200,000 Dth and 2,250,000 Dth effective Timely Cycle for Gas Day Thursday, March 5, 2026.

At this time, CGT does not anticipate an impact to nominations through CorinSEG.

CorinSEG
• Estimated Capacity Setting: 2,200,000 – 2,250,000 Dth
• Design Capacity: 2,540,656 Dth
• Posted Percentage: 87-89%

Due to the potential impact to firm service, CGT encourages customers to monitor the scheduled volumes on the Operationally Available Capacity screen on Informational Postings, as nominations can fluctuate based on market conditions.

Reservation Charge Credits (RCCs) will be determined per the process set forth in the General Terms and Conditions, Section 40 of CGT’s FERC Gas Tariff (Tariff).

Please contact your Customer Services Representative with any questions.

***Previous Posting***

Columbia Gulf Transmission, LLC (CGT) notifies customers that pigging on Mainline-200 from Corinth Compressor Station to Banner Compressor Station, previously scheduled to begin on January 31, 2026, has been rescheduled to March 5 – March 14, 2026.

Reservation Charge Credits (RCCs) will be determined per the process set forth in the General Terms and Conditions, Section 40 of CGT’s FERC Gas Tariff (Tariff).

Please contact your Customer Services Representative with any questions.

***Previous Posting***

Columbia Gulf Transmission, LLC (CGT) notifies customers that the pigging on Maineline-200 from Corinth Compressor Station to Banner Compressor Station is now scheduled to begin on Saturday, January 31, 2026 through Saturday, February 7, 2026.

Reservation Charge Credits (RCCs) will be determined per the process set forth in the General Terms and Conditions, Section 40 of CGT’s FERC Gas Tariff (Tariff).

Please contact your Customer Services Representative with any questions.

***Previous Posting***

Columbia Gulf Transmission, LLC (CGT) notifies customers that the pigging on Maineline-200 from Corinth Compressor Station to Banner Compressor Station originally scheduled to begin on January 22, 2026, has been rescheduled to Tuesday, January 27, 2026 through Wednesday, February 4, 2026.

Reservation Charge Credits (RCCs) will be determined per the process set forth in the General Terms and Conditions, Section 40 of CGT’s FERC Gas Tariff (Tariff).

Please contact your Customer Services Representative with any questions.

***Previous Posting***

Columbia Gulf Transmission LLC (CGT) notifies customers of upcoming pigging on Maineline-200 between Corinth Compressor Station and Banner Compressor Station beginning on Thursday January 22, 2026, through Friday January 30, 2026. As a result, the backhaul capacity through CorinSEG will be reduced to 2,200,000 Dth effective Timely Cycle for Gas Day Thursday, January 22, 2026.

At this time, CGT does not anticipate an impact to nominations through CorinSEG.

CorinSEG
• Estimated Capacity Setting: 2,200,000 Dth
• Design Capacity: 2,540,656 Dth
• Posted Percentage: 87 %

Due to the potential impact to firm service, CGT encourages customers to monitor the scheduled volumes on the Operationally Available Capacity screen on Informational Postings, as nominations can fluctuate based on market conditions.

Reservation Charge Credits (RCCs) will be determined per the process set forth in the General Terms and Conditions, Section 40 of CGT’s FERC Gas Tariff (Tariff). By means of this notice, CGT is establishing the 7-day Average Usage Quantity to be the period of December 30, 2025 – January 5, 2026, pursuant to the provisions in the Tariff.

Please contact your Customer Services Representative with any questions.
